diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Severe orthopnoea reflects gravity-dependent redistribution of pulmonary venous congestion.

symptoms.symptom_name: Missed diuretic dose and unused CPAP

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Identifiable precipitants for acute decompensation in a patient with known heart failure and OSA.

symptoms.symptom_name: Extreme exhaustion from work of breathing

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Marker of impending respiratory muscle fatigue and possible respiratory arrest.

symptoms.symptom_name: Bilateral fine crackles, S3 gallop, elevated JVP on examination

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Classic examination findings confirming left ventricular failure with fluid overload; clinician-only findings sealed from telehealth chat.

symptoms.symptom_name: Elevated BNP and cardiomegaly with Kerley B lines on chest radiograph

investigation_details.expected_result: Markedly elevated BNP and cardiomegaly with Kerley B lines confirming cardiogenic pulmonary oedema
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Diagnosis unconfirmed; telehealth cannot obtain this and should recommend it occur as part of emergency assessment.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Definitive supportive investigations for acute decompensated heart failure.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Profound orthopnoea, pink frothy sputum, and extreme respiratory exhaustion in a patient with known heart failure and identifiable precipitants together mandate immediate emergency ambulance dispatch with CPAP/BiPAP capability.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Non-invasive ventilation (CPAP/BiPAP), sublingual/IV nitrates, and IV diuretics
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Definitive emergency treatment for acute cardiogenic pulmonary oedema; requires in-person administration and monitoring.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Serum BNP, troponin, chest radiograph, and ECG
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms the diagnosis, assesses for a precipitating ischaemic event, and guides further management.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Outpatient echocardiogram as the first step tonight
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not appropriate as an acute first-line action; would dangerously delay emergency stabilisation.
